county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in West Union holds deeds from 1797. One of Ohio's original counties from the Northwest Territory. Located in the Virginia Military District — research Virginia military bounty land warrants at the Library of Virginia and BLM-GLO for original patents. Adams County was a major Underground Railroad corridor along the Ohio River; check the Rankin House records and local abolitionist histories. Serpent Mound area — early settlement records are sparse. Strong Scots-Irish heritage. Ohio History Connection has microfilmed county records.

source_profiles.repository: Adams County Recorder, West Union
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1797. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Adams County Probate Court, West Union
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1797. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Adams County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Adams County Probate Court, West Union
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1797. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Adams County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Adams County Clerk of Courts, West Union
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1797. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Adams County Auditor, West Union / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1797. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Adams County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Adams County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Lima. Organized 1831 (created 1820). Located in Congress Lands survey area. Lima became a major oil boom town in the 1880s — Lima Oil Field records at Ohio History Connection document thousands of workers and landowners. Strong German Catholic community; St. Rose, Glandorf, and Delphos parish records are essential. Allen County Historical Society maintains local collections. Miami & Erie Canal passed through — canal land records at state archives.

source_profiles.repository: Allen County Recorder, Lima
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Allen County Probate Court, Lima
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1820. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Allen County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Allen County Probate Court, Lima
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Allen County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Allen County Clerk of Courts, Lima
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1820. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Allen County Clerk of Courts, Lima / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection, Columbus
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1846 from Wayne, Richland, Huron, Lorain.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Ashland. Created 1846 from Wayne, Richland, Huron, and Lorain — check all four parent counties for pre-1846 records. Johnny Appleseed (John Chapman) territory; early orchard and nursery land records. Ashland University (originally Ashland College, 1878) archives hold local materials. Brethren church records important for German-heritage families. Congress Lands survey system.

source_profiles.repository: Ashland County Recorder, Ashland
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1846. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.repository: Ashland County Probate Court, Ashland
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1846. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Jefferson. Western Reserve county — early settlers predominantly from Connecticut and Massachusetts. Check Connecticut land records and Connecticut Western Reserve Land Company records at Western Reserve Historical Society (WRHS), Cleveland. Major Underground Railroad activity; Hubbard House in Ashtabula was a documented station. Covered Bridge capital of Ohio — township records document bridge construction workers. Finnish and Italian immigrant communities in Ashtabula Harbor and Conneaut (early 1900s). Geneva-on-the-Lake resort area records.

source_profiles.repository: Ashtabula County Recorder, Jefferson
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1807. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

source_profiles.source_name: Connecticut Western Reserve Land Records — Ashtabula County
source_profiles.repository: Western Reserve Historical Society, Cleveland / CT State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Connecticut ceded its Western Reserve to the federal government in 1800, but settlers arrived from 1796. Connecticut Land Company records document original purchasers and settlers. Check Connecticut state records for settler origins.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at WRHS in Cleveland; Connecticut State Library also has copies

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Ashtabula County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Athens. Ohio University (1804) is Ohio's oldest university — faculty, staff, and student records in Mahn Center archives. Located in the Ohio Company Purchase lands — original land grants at BLM-GLO. Coal mining region — mine employment records document immigrant workers. Athens County Historical Society and Museum. Early salt production records. Strong Methodist and Presbyterian church records from early settlement.

source_profiles.repository: Athens County Recorder, Athens
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1805. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio Company of Associates Land Records — Athens County
source_profiles.repository: Marietta College Archives / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Ohio Company of Associates (organized 1786 in Massachusetts) purchased 1.5 million acres in SE Ohio. Records document original purchasers, many from New England. Marietta was the first permanent settlement.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Marietta College Special Collections and Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.parent_county: Allen, Mercer, Darke, Shelby, Van Wert
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1848 from Allen, Mercer, Darke, Shelby, Van Wert.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Wapakoneta. Created 1848 from multiple parent counties — check Allen, Mercer, Darke, Shelby, and Van Wert for earlier records. Wapakoneta was Shawnee territory; check NARA for removal-era records. Neil Armstrong birthplace; space heritage has no genealogical relevance but local historical society is active. Very strong German Catholic heritage — St. Marys, Minster, New Bremen, and New Knoxville church records critical. Miami & Erie Canal corridor; Grand Reservoir (Lake St. Marys) construction records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.

source_profiles.source_name: German Catholic Parish Records — Auglaize County
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archdiocese of Cincinnati / Parish offices
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptism, marriage, and burial records from German Catholic parishes. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. Archdiocese of Cincinnati central archives hold some consolidated records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in St. Clairsville. One of Ohio's earliest counties (1801). Located on the National Road (US Route 40) — early tavern licenses and road construction records. Major coal mining county; mine records document immigrants especially Welsh, Scottish, and Eastern European. Quaker settlements in Barnesville and Mt. Pleasant — Friends meeting records at Ohio History Connection. Underground Railroad activity documented. Belmont County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1801. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Belmont County
source_profiles.repository: Wilmington College Quaker Heritage Center / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Quakers kept meticulous records. Ohio Yearly Meeting and Indiana Yearly Meeting records at Wilmington College.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed and indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Georgetown. Ulysses S. Grant birthplace (Point Pleasant) and boyhood home (Georgetown). Located in the Virginia Military District — VA bounty land warrants. Ripley on the Ohio River was a major Underground Railroad crossing point; John Rankin and John Parker records. Strong tobacco farming heritage — crop lien and agricultural records. Brown County Genealogical Society is active. Early Baptist and Methodist circuit rider records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Hamilton (city). Miami University (1809) archives in Oxford hold local materials. Located partly in Symmes Purchase (Between the Miami Rivers) — John Cleves Symmes land records. Hamilton is a major industrial city; Beckett Paper Company and other factory employment records. Strong German immigrant community. Butler County Historical Society. Great Miami River flood records (1913 and earlier) affected families and property.

source_profiles.source_name: Symmes Purchase Land Records — Butler County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Cincinnati History Library and Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: John Cleves Symmes purchased approximately 1 million acres between the Great Miami and Little Miami Rivers. Records document original patent holders and early settlers in SW Ohio.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records at Ohio History Connection and Cincinnati History Library and Archives

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / NARA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1832 from Columbiana, Stark, Harrison, Tuscarawas, Jefferson.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Carrollton. Created 1832 from five parent counties — check Columbiana, Stark, Harrison, Tuscarawas, and Jefferson for earlier records. Named for Charles Carroll, last surviving signer of the Declaration of Independence. Recent Utica shale oil/gas boom brought new land records. Rural agricultural county — township records and school district records are important supplements. McCook House Civil War Museum holds military records. Sandy and Beaver Canal records.

source_profiles.repository: Carroll County Recorder, Carrollton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1832. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
